headline: France's political parties still don't know how to select their presidential candidates
description: With the local elections finished, the race for the presidency is kicking off. Except for the far right and the radical left, parties now face the pressing question of how to select their candidates for the 2027 presidential election.

headline: Macron visits Japan and South Korea, both worried about war in Iran
French channel CNews is accused of allowing racist remarks against mayor
Israel introduces death penalty for Palestinians accused of 'terrorist' murders
description: The law, passed by 62 lawmakers to 48, marks turning point in Israel's penal policy and further weakens the rule of law in the country.

headline: 'The Middle East war could trigger the private credit crisis that has been brewing for months'
description: The fundamentals of the debt sector funded by non-bank lenders are weakening, as regulators have allowed this market to develop outside their line of sight, writes Le Monde columnist Stéphane Lauer.

headline: Spice up your life! Five restaurants in the Paris region where you can eat spicy food
description: Mexican chipotle, Korean kimchi, Tunisian harissa: Whether mild or fiery, these condiments add zest to dishes from around the world. Here are five addresses in Paris and Montreuil to awaken your taste buds.

headline: War is transforming Ukraine's dogs who live on the front line
description: Canines found near combat zones are small, light and dark-colored, according to a study. These characteristics have helped them survive in a hostile environment.

Christian Jambet, Islamic scholar: 'The true leaders of Iran are now soldiers who want to spread the Islamic Revolution beyond Iran's border'
description: The philosopher and member of the Académie Française, an expert on Shiite spiritualities, shares his thoughts on the ongoing war and the 'degeneration' of the Iranian regime.

headline: Escaping Putin's Russia: The father and daughter persecuted over a drawing
description: Alexei Moskalyov and his daughter, Maria, are symbolic victims of the Kremlin's repression. The father was jailed after posting an anti-war drawing his teenage daughter made at school. They have just arrived in France and are under humanitarian protection.

headline: Assault, attempted murder, assassination: The rogue Freemasons going on trial in Paris
description: A coach, a race car driver, a trade unionist: Nothing seemed to connect these otherwise unremarkable individuals. Yet all were targeted by the same criminal organization. At its head were prominent members of a Masonic lodge in a Paris suburb. These 'brothers,' now enemies, are standing trial from March 30.

headline: France has yet to find a forward-looking political discourse on racism

Iranians are caught in the trap of Trump's incoherence
